data.name: ExploreSubtitle
data.value: Swipe the cards and choose a category below.

data.name: FavoritesSearchPlaceholder
data.value: Search by name, category, or description

data.name: FavoritesHint
data.value: Pick a place to remove it.

data.name: FavoriteSaved
data.value: The place was saved to favorites.

data.name: ToompeaShort
data.value: A powerful castle complex above Tallinn Old Town.

data.name: ToompeaDescription
data.value: Toompea Castle is a symbol of Estonian statehood and one of Tallinn's oldest defensive landmarks. Nearby viewing platforms give a clear view of Old Town towers and the harbor.

data.name: NevskyShort
data.value: A striking Orthodox cathedral in the heart of Toompea.

data.name: NevskyDescription
data.value: Alexander Nevsky Cathedral is known for its onion domes, mosaics, and resonant interior. It remains one of the most recognizable landmarks on Toompea Hill.

data.name: KadriorgShort
data.value: A historic park with a palace, alleys, and ponds.

data.name: KadriorgDescription
data.value: Kadriorg Park brings together a baroque palace, art museums, fountains, and quiet walking paths. It works well for both a short pause and a longer walk.

data.name: JapaneseGardenShort
data.value: A calm garden shaped by stone, water, and seasonal plants.

data.name: JapaneseGardenDescription
data.value: The Japanese Garden in Kadriorg is designed for slow walks and composed views. It is especially expressive during spring blooming and autumn color.

data.name: OpenAirMuseumShort
data.value: An excellent open-air museum showcasing traditional Estonian farmhouses and landscapes.

data.name: OpenAirMuseumDescription
data.value: The Estonian Open Air Museum presents centuries of rural Estonian life and architecture. It features historic village buildings, parkland, and cultural events that celebrate folk traditions.

data.name: NarvaCastleShort
data.value: An ancient fortress on the Narva River, standing at the Estonian-Russian border.

data.name: NarvaCastleDescription
data.value: Narva Castle is one of Estonia's oldest and most strategically important fortifications. Over 800 years old, the castle offers views of the Narva River and the opposite shore.

data.name: KumuShort
data.value: A contemporary art museum in Kadriorg, the heart of Estonian art.

data.name: KumuDescription
data.value: Kumu Art Museum showcases Estonian art from the 18th century to the present day. The museum building itself is a work of art set in the heart of Kadriorg Park.

data.name: LahemaaShort
data.value: A beautiful national park with coastline, forests, and bogs.

data.name: LahemaaDescription
data.value: Lahemaa National Park is Estonia's largest and one of its oldest national parks, known for forests, coastal landscapes, and manor houses. It is ideal for nature hikes and seaside walks.

data.name: ParnuBeachShort
data.value: A popular sandy beach and holiday destination in southern Estonia.

data.name: ParnuBeachDescription
data.value: Pärnu Beach is known for soft sand, a lively promenade, and vibrant summer atmosphere. It offers many activities and leisure options during the season.

data.name: OldeHansaShort
data.value: A medieval-style restaurant in Tallinn Old Town.

data.name: OldeHansaDescription
data.value: Olde Hansa serves dishes inspired by medieval Tallinn, with candlelight and historical service style. It suits visitors looking for dinner with a distinctive atmosphere.

data.name: RataskaevuShort
data.value: A cozy Old Town restaurant with local food and warm service.

data.name: RataskaevuDescription
data.value: Rataskaevu 16 is known for friendly service, a seasonal menu, and simple quality cooking. Its location makes it a natural pause while exploring Old Town.
